findings.explanation: A percentage is bounded by definition to 0%–100% (1 above the logical maximum of 100%, 1 below the logical minimum of 0%). For example, a value of 150% exceeds the logical maximum of 100% for a percentage column; and a value of -5% is below the logical minimum of 0% (a percentage cannot be negative) — impossible by definition and most likely a decimal-point slip, unit mix-up, or data-entry typo rather than a real value.
findings.text: 2 value(s) in "completion_pct" fall outside the logical percentage range of 0%–100%.
